title: Find Eigenvalues using Power Iteration
description: Power iteration method to find dominant eigenvalue and eigenvector
code:
// Power Iteration Algorithm
// Finds dominant eigenvalue and corresponding eigenvector

PROCEDURE PowerIteration(A, tolerance, maxIterations)
 BEGIN
 n ← A.rows
 // Random initial vector
 v ← [1, 1, ..., 1] // unit vector
 eigenvalue_old ← 0
 
 FOR k ← 1 TO maxIterations DO
 // Multiply matrix by vector
 w ← A * v
 
 // Normalize to prevent overflow
 eigenvalue ← max(abs(w))
 v ← w / eigenvalue
 
 // Check convergence
 IF |eigenvalue - eigenvalue_old| < tolerance THEN
 BREAK
 END IF
 eigenvalue_old ← eigenvalue
 END FOR
 
 RETURN eigenvalue, v
 END PROCEDURE
explanation: Power iteration repeatedly multiplies matrix A by a vector and normalizes. It converges to the dominant eigenvector. The eigenvalue is the scaling factor each iteration.

complexity.time: O(n² × k) where k is iterations

title: Verify Cayley-Hamilton Theorem
description: Algorithm to verify CHT by computing characteristic polynomial and substituting matrix A
explanation: Computes p(A) where p(λ) is the characteristic polynomial and verifies it equals zero matrix. Uses formula for 2×2 case for simplicity.

title: Diagonalize Matrix using Eigendecomposition
description: Find P and D such that A = PDP⁻¹ using eigenvalue decomposition
code:
// Diagonalization Algorithm
// A = PDP⁻¹ where D = eigenvalues, P = eigenvectors

PROCEDURE Diagonalize(A)
 BEGIN
 n ← A.rows
 
 // Find all eigenvalues (using QR algorithm or direct method)
 eigenvalues ← FindEigenvalues(A)
 
 // Find corresponding eigenvectors
 eigenvectors ← EmptyMatrix(n, n)
 
 FOR i ← 1 TO n DO
 λ ← eigenvalues[i]
 // Solve (A - λI)v = 0
 M ← MatrixSubtract(A, ScalarMultiply(IdentityMatrix(n), λ))
 v ← NullSpaceVector(M) // Find non-trivial solution
 eigenvectors[*, i] ← v
 END FOR
 
 // Form P and D
 P ← eigenvectors
 D ← DiagonalMatrix(eigenvalues)
 
 // Verify diagonalization
 P_inv ← MatrixInverse(P)
 reconstructed ← MatrixMultiply(MatrixMultiply(P, D), P_inv)
 
 IF MatrixEquals(reconstructed, A, tolerance) THEN
 RETURN P, D, "Diagonalization successful"
 ELSE
 RETURN NULL, NULL, "Matrix not diagonalizable"
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE
explanation: Eigendecomposition: finds all eigenvalues and eigenvectors, forms diagonal matrix D with eigenvalues and matrix P with eigenvectors as columns. Verifies A = PDP⁻¹.
